title: The Flirt Episode

jokes.text: That sign says 'applesauce.' No, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Warm-up comedian points to applause sign
jokes.punchline: Fake-out with similar sounding word 'applesauce'
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection warm-up joke. Competent but predictable setup-punchline structure.

dialogue.line: That sign says 'applesauce.' No, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'

jokes.text: Really. That's... You're very desperate.
jokes.setup: Larry acknowledging audience applause
jokes.punchline: Deadpan assessment of their enthusiasm as desperation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders character moment - paranoid and insulting his own audience with brutal honesty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Really. That's... You're very desperate.

jokes.text: Princess Di, it was announced in the paper today... has, evidently, rumored, I guess, bulimia. And this is true, I'm going to England. Hank, maybe you can help me on this, 'cause I'm not up on the protocol. I know you're not supposed to eat until they eat. Now, how would it work in this case?
jokes.setup: Princess Diana's bulimia announcement and British dining protocol
jokes.punchline: How do you follow 'don't eat until they eat' etiquette with someone who has bulimia
jokes.explanation: Dark, topical humor that was edgy for its time. Smart premise connecting protocol with eating disorder.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Princess Di, it was announced in the paper today... has, evidently, rumored, I guess, bulimia. And this is true, I'm going to England. Hank, maybe you can help me on this, 'cause I'm not up on the protocol. I know you're not supposed to eat until they eat. Now, how would it work in this case?

jokes.text: All I know is always use the small fork first.
jokes.setup: Larry's complex bulimia etiquette question
jokes.punchline: Hank's completely unhelpful generic etiquette advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character moment - missing the point entirely with basic etiquette trivia.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: All I know is always use the small fork first.

jokes.text: Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Hank's unhelpful fork advice
jokes.punchline: Larry's deflated realization of Hank's uselessness
jokes.explanation: Economy of disappointment - two words convey Larry's exasperation perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, boy.

jokes.text: 'Keep the day job' is the old joke.
jokes.setup: His own failed joke about Princess Diana
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his own bombing
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about bad late-night TV jokes. Shows Larry's self-awareness of his mediocrity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: 'Keep the day job' is the old joke.

jokes.text: Ross Perot has dropped out of the presidential race... proving again that if you have a silent T in your last name... you cannot become president. Paul Tsongas... The silent T does not work. Harry Truman, yes... because his name isn't Harry Ruman.
jokes.setup: Political observation about Ross Perot dropping out
jokes.punchline: Absurd theory about silent T's preventing presidency, with Truman counterexample
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist take on politics. The Truman/Ruman bit shows commitment to the absurd premise.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Ross Perot has dropped out of the presidential race... proving again that if you have a silent T in your last name... you cannot become president. Paul Tsongas... The silent T does not work. Harry Truman, yes... because his name isn't Harry Ruman.

jokes.text: We have a wonderful show. Don't judge it by the jokes.
jokes.setup: Standard 'wonderful show' setup line
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating acknowledgment of his weak material
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary. Larry admits his monologue is weak while doing the monologue. Perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: We have a wonderful show. Don't judge it by the jokes.

jokes.text: I would have voted for you, too, if you'd been in a movie.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he would have voted for her for an Academy Award
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious flaw in his hypothetical scenario
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that highlights Larry's thoughtless compliment. Clean structure.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I would have voted for you, too, if you'd been in a movie.

jokes.text: That's the prerequisite that's stopped me from winning an Academy Award. There's some little, small print that says... you have to be in a movie to get a nomination.
jokes.setup: Acknowledging he's never been in a movie
jokes.punchline: Treating this obvious requirement as unfair fine print
jokes.explanation: Larry's delusional self-importance. Acts like being in movies is a technicality preventing his Oscar.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's the prerequisite that's stopped me from winning an Academy Award. There's some little, small print that says... you have to be in a movie to get a nomination.

jokes.text: Among other things, I would love to see how you do a nude scene.
jokes.setup: Innocent movie collaboration suggestion
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual interest disguised as professional curiosity
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry creepy behavior. The 'among other things' makes it worse by pretending it's casual.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Among other things, I would love to see how you do a nude scene.

jokes.text: I do them... I can tell you, funny and yet sexy.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about her nude scene approach
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of impossible combination
jokes.explanation: Actress playing along with inappropriate question. The contradiction of funny yet sexy is mildly amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Mimi Rogers
dialogue.line: I do them... I can tell you, funny and yet sexy.

jokes.text: I'm an Aquarius. I think that would work. Is that right? Sure. You're a fire sign. I'm an air sign. Air feeds the fire, fans the fire, strokes the fire.
jokes.setup: Astrological compatibility discussion
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual metaphors disguised as astrology
jokes.explanation: Larry using astrology as flirtation vehicle. The escalating verbs (feeds, fans, strokes) show his building excitement.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm an Aquarius. I think that would work. Is that right? Sure. You're a fire sign. I'm an air sign. Air feeds the fire, fans the fire, strokes the fire.

jokes.text: Easy.
jokes.setup: Larry's increasingly sexual fire metaphors
jokes.punchline: One-word attempt to cool him down
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word conveys her attempt to dial back Larry's intensity.

dialogue.line: Easy.

jokes.text: The harder one blows the air into the fire, the more... Flame, yeah. Heat, and... Explosions can result. And before you know it, there's no way to put it out.
jokes.setup: Continued fire/air sexual metaphor
jokes.punchline: Increasingly obvious sexual innuendo reaching climax with 'explosions' and inability to 'put it out'
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy escalation. Larry completely lost in his metaphor, Mimi helpfully finishing his thoughts.

dialogue.line: The harder one blows the air into the fire, the more... Flame, yeah. Heat, and... Explosions can result. And before you know it, there's no way to put it out.

jokes.text: And my rising sign is kindling, but, please... let's get back to the nude scene.
jokes.setup: Extended astrology/fire metaphor
jokes.punchline: Fake astrological term followed by blunt return to sexual topic
jokes.explanation: 'Rising sign is kindling' is absurdist gold. Then Larry drops all pretense and returns to his real interest.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And my rising sign is kindling, but, please... let's get back to the nude scene.

jokes.text: I'm a Capricorn.
jokes.setup: Larry and Mimi's sexual astrology discussion
jokes.punchline: Hank awkwardly inserting himself into intimate conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - completely missing social cues and inappropriately joining in.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm a Capricorn.

jokes.text: Butt out.
jokes.setup: Hank inserting himself into the conversation
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal of his sidekick
jokes.explanation: Larry's territorial reaction to Hank interrupting his flirtation. Economical and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Butt out.

jokes.text: I can tell you that that has never stopped him before.
jokes.setup: Mimi saying she's married
jokes.punchline: Hank revealing Larry's history with married women
jokes.explanation: Hank undermining Larry by revealing his pattern of infidelity. Dark character humor.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I can tell you that that has never stopped him before.

jokes.text: Now, by nude, do you mean neither of us would be wearing clothes?
jokes.setup: Previous nude scene discussion and marriage declaration
jokes.punchline: Clarifying obvious definition while ignoring marriage obstacle
jokes.explanation: Larry's one-track mind. Asks technical questions about nudity while ignoring her marriage.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Now, by nude, do you mean neither of us would be wearing clothes?

jokes.text: Because I've been tricked before and ended up being the only one. Usually it results in a call to the police and all hell breaks out.
jokes.setup: Clarifying mutual nudity
jokes.punchline: Revealing history of one-sided nude incidents and legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation revealing Larry's inappropriate past behavior and legal troubles.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Because I've been tricked before and ended up being the only one. Usually it results in a call to the police and all hell breaks out.

jokes.text: That's the first time you've invited anyone back for two nights in a row. And that includes Hank.
jokes.setup: Larry inviting Mimi back tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Highlighting this unprecedented gesture and Hank's exclusion
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Larry's unusual behavior and Hank's status.

dialogue.line: That's the first time you've invited anyone back for two nights in a row. And that includes Hank.

jokes.text: What an asshole.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing for running out of time
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment from the bumped guest
jokes.explanation: Michael Richards's frustrated reaction. Economy and honesty make it work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Richards
dialogue.line: What an asshole.

jokes.text: Why is he inviting her back? I'm the one who got bumped.
jokes.setup: Larry inviting Mimi back for second night
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious unfairness to him as bumped guest
jokes.explanation: Logical complaint from Michael Richards about Larry's obvious favoritism.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Richards
dialogue.line: Why is he inviting her back? I'm the one who got bumped.

jokes.text: Jazzercise.
jokes.setup: Asked how she comes down from doing a show
jokes.punchline: Mundane '80s exercise trend as post-show routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan unexpected answer. The specificity of 'Jazzercise' makes it funnier than generic 'exercise.'

dialogue.speaker: Mimi Rogers
dialogue.line: Jazzercise.

jokes.text: No, but glad to hear the Russian lessons are working out for you.
jokes.setup: Someone saying they're going for a 'drinkski'
jokes.punchline: Mocking the fake Russian suffix
jokes.explanation: Good callback to mock someone's affected speech pattern. Clean sarcastic response.

dialogue.line: No, but glad to hear the Russian lessons are working out for you.

jokes.text: Try the veal. I don't care that they live in cages. It's out of this world.
jokes.setup: Restaurant recommendation
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging and dismissing animal cruelty for taste
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about food ethics. The casual dismissal of cruelty is mildly shocking.

dialogue.line: Try the veal. I don't care that they live in cages. It's out of this world.

jokes.text: You know, if Hank does well he gets a year's supply of Turtle Wax.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Hank's performance
jokes.punchline: Game show prize as reward for talk show sidekick
jokes.explanation: Absurd reward system treating Hank like game show contestant. Good specificity with Turtle Wax.

dialogue.line: You know, if Hank does well he gets a year's supply of Turtle Wax.

jokes.text: So, do you fuck her on the desk or on the couch? I'm guessing the desk because Hank is on the couch... and I know you're not into that.
jokes.setup: Larry's wife watching his flirtatious interview
jokes.punchline: Blunt sexual questioning with logistics and Hank reference
jokes.explanation: Shocking directness from wife. The desk/couch logistics and Hank reference make it specifically funny rather than just crude.

dialogue.speaker: Jeannie
dialogue.line: So, do you fuck her on the desk or on the couch? I'm guessing the desk because Hank is on the couch... and I know you're not into that.

jokes.text: It'll be George Segal month.
jokes.setup: Discussion of setting bad precedent by inviting guests back
jokes.punchline: Specific actor representing unwanted repeat booking outcome
jokes.explanation: Perfect showbiz insider reference. George Segal as the nightmare of endless bookings is specific and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It'll be George Segal month.

jokes.text: We've got nothing.
jokes.setup: Writer's elaborate Batman sketch setup
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment of the material
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy writing room moment. Three words that deflate the entire elaborate pitch.

dialogue.line: We've got nothing.

jokes.text: The heavy one has such a pretty face.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the Bangles reuniting
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment mixing up bands
jokes.explanation: Artie's crude assessment showing he's confused about which band is which. Typical character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The heavy one has such a pretty face.

jokes.text: Actually, kindling is my rising sign. So we could build quite a fire there.
jokes.setup: Watching previous night's tape
jokes.punchline: Calling back to his fake astrological term from the interview
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier absurdist line. Shows Larry's continued self-delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Actually, kindling is my rising sign. So we could build quite a fire there.

jokes.text: This is like watching the Rodney King tape. Don't make me watch this anymore.
jokes.setup: Watching Larry's flirtatious interview repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Comparing romantic cringe to police brutality footage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comparison. Artie equating Larry's cringe with traumatic news footage is perfectly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: This is like watching the Rodney King tape. Don't make me watch this anymore.

jokes.text: I have seen the hand on the hand done many times. I, myself, have done it with Gregory Peck. Trust me, I felt nothing.
jokes.setup: Larry analyzing Mimi's flirtatious hand gesture
jokes.punchline: Artie's experience with same gesture involving classic movie star
jokes.explanation: Excellent Artie moment. Gregory Peck reference shows his Hollywood history while deflating Larry's paranoia with deadpan authority.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I have seen the hand on the hand done many times. I, myself, have done it with Gregory Peck. Trust me, I felt nothing.

jokes.text: All I'm saying is I've played this course before. I know right where it goes. Hand on hand, flirting backstage... and then I'm fuckin' 'em in Hank's Malibu pad.
jokes.setup: Larry's analysis of his flirtation pattern
jokes.punchline: Golf metaphor leading to crude admission about using Hank's place for affairs
jokes.explanation: Larry's self-awareness of his pattern is funny, plus using Hank's place adds insult to injury.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: All I'm saying is I've played this course before. I know right where it goes. Hand on hand, flirting backstage... and then I'm fuckin' 'em in Hank's Malibu pad.

jokes.text: You don't want to do that. He's had septic problems with that place.
jokes.setup: Larry talking about using Hank's Malibu pad for affairs
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about plumbing issues over moral ones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie response - practical concerns trump moral ones. Septic problems as deal-breaker is absurdly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You don't want to do that. He's had septic problems with that place.

jokes.text: Tell them I have Dutch elm disease.
jokes.setup: Tree People benefit invitation
jokes.punchline: Using tree disease as excuse to avoid tree-related charity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist excuse. Using tree disease to avoid tree charity shows Larry's creative selfishness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tell them I have Dutch elm disease.

jokes.text: Leno will be jealous. I think he's just got that has-been Julia Roberts on.
jokes.setup: Booking soap carver Bert Crawley
jokes.punchline: Calling Julia Roberts a has-been while celebrating booking unknown soap carver
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry delusion. Dismissing A-list actress as has-been while celebrating terrible booking shows his complete lack of perspective.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Leno will be jealous. I think he's just got that has-been Julia Roberts on.

jokes.text: I am not gonna ask her about when she lost her virginity... because then I'll have to tell mine, and it always embarrasses my sister.
jokes.setup: List of interview questions to avoid
jokes.punchline: Implication of incestuous relationship with sister
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that gets more disturbing the more you think about it. The casualness makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I am not gonna ask her about when she lost her virginity... because then I'll have to tell mine, and it always embarrasses my sister.

jokes.text: Always embarrasses my sister, I say. Never mind. I get it.
jokes.setup: His previous incest implication
jokes.punchline: Awkward recovery attempt that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe follow-up. Larry trying to backtrack from his inappropriate comment only makes it more awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Always embarrasses my sister, I say. Never mind. I get it.

jokes.text: It seems these two colored fellas were sittin' on the porch... I don't think there's going to be enough time tonight. But this is a very funny joke! It'll make their hair giggle! I'm sure it's great. It doesn't have to be colored. We can make them Polack.
jokes.setup: Soap carver wanting to tell jokes on TV
jokes.punchline: Racist joke setup with casual offer to change ethnicities
jokes.explanation: Cringe character comedy showing Bert's outdated sensibilities. The casual ethnic substitution adds to the discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Bert Crawley
dialogue.line: It seems these two colored fellas were sittin' on the porch... I don't think there's going to be enough time tonight. But this is a very funny joke! It'll make their hair giggle! I'm sure it's great. It doesn't have to be colored. We can make them Polack.

jokes.text: Oprah's actually a small, white Jewish man. But don't tell anybody.
jokes.setup: Discussing makeup and talk show hosts
jokes.punchline: Conspiracy theory about Oprah's true identity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist conspiracy theory from Hank. The specificity and earnest delivery make it perfect character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oprah's actually a small, white Jewish man. But don't tell anybody.

jokes.text: You're such a good actress that I thought you were serious for a second.
jokes.setup: Mimi admitting her flirtation was serious
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate attempt to reframe it as acting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry panic response. Trying to convince himself it was performance when confronted with real attraction.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're such a good actress that I thought you were serious for a second.

jokes.text: Are you okay? I have a rotator cuff problem. I threw it out during the monologue.
jokes.setup: Larry stretching or moving awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Blaming physical comedy injury on his weak monologue performance
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy premise. The idea of injuring himself during his own bad monologue is absurd and in character.

dialogue.line: Are you okay?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I have a rotator cuff problem. I threw it out during the monologue.

jokes.text: You look so much thinner in person.
jokes.setup: Meeting someone in person
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about weight
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate comment that reveals Bert's social cluelessness.

dialogue.speaker: Bert Crawley
dialogue.line: You look so much thinner in person.

jokes.text: There's these two Jews sitting on the porch. One of them says to the other...
jokes.setup: Bert attempting another ethnic joke
jokes.punchline: Switching to different ethnic group for his inappropriate joke
jokes.explanation: Callback to Bert's pattern of inappropriate ethnic humor. The persistence shows his character but isn't particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Bert Crawley
dialogue.line: There's these two Jews sitting on the porch. One of them says to the other...

jokes.text: That's your philtrum. I know, 'cause it's very hard to cover up.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the groove under nose
jokes.punchline: Specific anatomical term and makeup-related concern
jokes.explanation: Good Hank moment showing his makeup knowledge. Philtrum is specific and the cover-up concern is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That's your philtrum. I know, 'cause it's very hard to cover up.

jokes.text: Usually you take the keys by the third try.
jokes.setup: Larry refusing keys to Hank's Malibu place
jokes.punchline: Hank's knowledge of Larry's usual affair pattern
jokes.explanation: Great Hank observation about Larry's patterns. Shows their relationship dynamic and Larry's predictability.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Usually you take the keys by the third try.

jokes.text: That? I'm not gonna see that. You do that all day long anyway.
jokes.setup: Arthur suggesting a signal for when Larry is flirting
jokes.punchline: Larry pointing out Arthur's constant gesturing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Arthur's habitual gesturing making signals useless.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That? I'm not gonna see that. You do that all day long anyway.

jokes.text: You are completely adorable when you can't take a compliment.
jokes.setup: Larry's awkward response to compliment
jokes.punchline: Meta-observation about his inability to handle praise
jokes.explanation: Good character observation. Mimi recognizing Larry's neurotic patterns and finding them endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Mimi Rogers
dialogue.line: You are completely adorable when you can't take a compliment.

jokes.text: 'Camptown Races.' I know that song. Sounds good to me.
jokes.setup: Awkward situation with Mimi and soap carver
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate enthusiasm for terrible entertainment option
jokes.explanation: Larry's desperation to avoid flirting leads to embracing terrible entertainment. Shows his panic state.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: 'Camptown Races.' I know that song. Sounds good to me.

jokes.text: She's singing 'Camptown Races' with a guy who carves soap. She's just being a good guest.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about Mimi's intentions
jokes.punchline: Artie's rational assessment of the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie voice of reason. Describing the absurd situation matter-of-factly shows how ridiculous Larry's jealousy is.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: She's singing 'Camptown Races' with a guy who carves soap. She's just being a good guest.

jokes.text: Michelle Pfeiffer?
jokes.setup: Larry announcing Michelle Pfeiffer as tomorrow's guest
jokes.punchline: Surprised reaction suggesting this booking is unlikely/impossible
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Larry's obviously false announcement. Two words that convey disbelief at his desperate lie.

dialogue.line: Michelle Pfeiffer?

tmdb_overview: Jeannie becomes very jealous and accuses Larry of flirting with a playful Mimi Rogers on the show.
